name: MiniMax-M1
archDisplay: Hybrid Lightning+Softmax Attention MoE (80 layers, 32 experts top-2) reasoning model, trained with CISPO RL

price.provider: MiniMax (platform.minimax.io, 0-200K token input tier; 200K-1M tier is $1.3/$2.2)

note: World's first open-weight large-scale hybrid-attention reasoning model; shipped as two checkpoints (40K/80K thinking budget) - this entry reflects the flagship 80K checkpoint.
tpsNote: llama.cpp did not support the MiniMaxM1ForCausalLM architecture at release, so no GGUF/Q4 quant or RTX 4090 figure exists.

results.notes: TAU-bench retail, M1-80K checkpoint (M1-40K scores 67.8 on the same task). Cross-confirmed identically on the official HF model card and the github.com/MiniMax-AI/MiniMax-M1 README.
MATH-500, M1-80K checkpoint (M1-40K: 96.0). Maps to the taxonomy's 'math' slug, which is itself defined as '500 competition math problems'.

HLE (Humanity's Last Exam), text-only subset, no tools, M1-80K checkpoint (the corpus's tracked flagship checkpoint) — M1-40K scores 7.2 on the same eval. Confirmed identically on both the official HF model card benchmark table and the MiniMax-AI/MiniMax-M1 GitHub README benchmark table. Not previously tracked in this corpus entry (only AIME 2024, LiveCodeBench, SWE-bench, GPQA, MMLU-Pro, TAU-bench, SimpleQA, and MATH were tracked).

verificationNotes: Confirmed real via VentureBeat, Computerworld, arXiv 2506.13585, and HF model card MiniMaxAI/MiniMax-M1-80k. 456B/45.9B hybrid MoE, Apache-2.0, 1M context confirmed by multiple independent sources. All 5 result rows confirmed literally on the HF card, including the M1-80K vs M1-40K split (AIME 86.0, LCB 65.0, SWE-bench 56.0 for 80K; GPQA 69.2, MMLU-Pro 80.6 for 40K) — exact match, no changes. Pricing $0.4/$2.2 for 0-200K tier independently confirmed.
